segments.text: Look at this expensive crystal deal. The only ingredient, potassium alum. The same stuff that you can buy for pennies.
So, is it a scam? Not really. They have solved three massive design problems.
First, raw alum is slippery. When you use it wet after shaving, it slips, falls, and shatters almost instantly.
So, premium brands added a simple cork or wooden handle. Suddenly, it's not a wet rock anymore. It's a precision tool that never slips. But there was a bigger
problem. If you leave wet alum on the shelf, it dissolves and leave a salty,
sticky mess. So, they designed these ventilated drip dry cases. It lets the air in to dry the stove, but keeps the water off your counter. So, are you
still overpaying? Technically, yes. But you aren't paying for the rock. You're paying for the grip, the case, and the dignity of not having a slimy stone in
your fancy bathroom. That's actually smart. Subscribe for more.
